tasks.description: Phase 1 Step 1: Audit useTimelineSave internal dependency graph. Map the three concern clusters (save/persist, poll, commit/edit) and document shared refs across them. This produces the interface contracts for the three new modules.
tasks.executor_notes: Audited `useTimelineSave.ts` and documented the three concern clusters, their dependency graph, all shared refs crossing the cluster boundaries, and proposed acyclic interfaces for `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` in `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/restructure-the-video-editor-20260406-0206/t1-useTimelineSave-audit.md`. Verified the audit explicitly captures the commit<->persistence bridge, the poll/sync dependencies on save refs, and the shared-ref contract needed for the next extraction step.

tasks.description:
Phase 1 Steps 2-4: Extract useTimelinePersistence, usePollSync, and useTimelineCommit from useTimelineSave. Create three new files:
- `hooks/useTimelinePersistence.ts` — scheduleSave, doSave, loadConflictRetryVersion, reloadFromServer, retrySaveAfterConflict, saveStatus, isConflictExhausted, isSaving
- `hooks/usePollSync.ts` — getPollRejectionReason (inverted to default-reject via isIdle whitelist: savedSeq >= editSeq && !isSaving && pendingOpsRef === 0), poll acceptance effect, shouldAcceptPolledData integration
- `hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts` — commitData, applyTimelineEdit, applyResolvedConfigEdit, materializeData, data/dataRef state, selection state, editSeqRef, pendingOpsRef

CRITICAL: pendingOpsRef must remain exactly as-is — both useClipDrag (line 434) and useAssetOperations (lines 19-37) increment/decrement it. The isIdle formula includes pendingOpsRef === 0 as one condition. No caller behavior changes.

Preserve both applyTimelineEdit and applyResolvedConfigEdit as-is in this step — unification happens in Phase 2.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync`, then rewired `useTimelineSave` to compose the shared refs and preserve the downstream interface. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 21 files / 62 tests, and `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed. Added `usePollSync.test.ts` to cover the `editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOps` matrix: all non-saving cases match the legacy accept/reject behavior, and the only deliberate new rejection is the approved `isSaving=true && savedSeq>=editSeq && pendingOpsRef===0` default-reject gate.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description:
Phase 1 Step 5: Slim useTimelineSave to thin composer. Reduce useTimelineSave.ts to a composition shell calling useTimelineCommit + useTimelinePersistence + usePollSync, returning the identical interface. Preserve all existing exports (SaveStatus, shouldAcceptPolledData, the useTimelineSave function signature) so no downstream imports break.

Run tests: `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`
Run tsc: `cd reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit`
All must pass with zero behavioral change.
tasks.executor_notes: Kept `useTimelineSave.ts` as a thin composition shell and tightened the interface hygiene by reintroducing a local `UseTimelineSaveQueries` alias and explicitly typing the bridged `scheduleSaveRef` as `ScheduleSaveFn`. Verification passed with `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` (21 files / 62 tests), `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit`, and a targeted ESLint pass across the changed files.

tasks.description:
Phase 3 Step 8: Extract types and re-exports into useTimelineData.types.ts. Create `hooks/useTimelineData.types.ts` containing:
- UseTimelineDataResult interface (currently at useTimelineData.ts:24-84)
- Re-exports: SaveStatus from ./useTimelineSave, RenderStatus from ./useRenderState, ClipTab and EditorPreferences from ./useEditorPreferences

Explicitly DROP the shouldAcceptPolledData re-export (line 20 of useTimelineData.ts) — grep confirms zero consumers import it from this path.

Update ALL 13+ importers to import types from useTimelineData.types instead of useTimelineData:
- useTimelineState.types.ts:5, useExternalDrop.ts:11, useClipDrag.ts:5, useClipResize.ts:5, useClipEditing.ts:17, useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:9, useAssetManagement.ts:17, useStaleVariants.ts:5, bulk-utils.ts:1, BulkClipPanel.tsx:19, ClipPanel.tsx:16, useTimelineState.ts:23

Update useTimelineState.types.ts:12 — change TimelineDataHook = ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData> to use explicit UseTimelineDataResult.

Run tsc --noEmit to verify all imports compile.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.types.ts`, moved `UseTimelineDataResult` into the shared types module, re-exported `SaveStatus`, `RenderStatus`, `ClipTab`, and `EditorPreferences` there, and dropped the unused `shouldAcceptPolledData` re-export from `useTimelineData.ts`. Updated the planned importer sites to use `useTimelineData.types.ts`, including replacing `ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData>` with the explicit `UseTimelineDataResult` type in `useTimelineState.types.ts`. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly.

tasks.description:
Phase 3 Step 9: Inline useTimelineData orchestration into useTimelineState, then delete useTimelineData.ts.

In useTimelineState.ts, replace `const dataHook = useTimelineData()` with direct calls to sub-hooks:
- useEditorPreferences(timelineId)
- useTimelineQueries(provider, timelineId)
- useTimelineSave(queries, provider, onSaveSuccessRef, pruneSelectionRef)
- useTimelineHistory({ dataRef, commitData })
- useDerivedTimeline(save.data, save.selectedClipId, save.selectedTrackId) — first call
- useRenderState(derived.resolvedConfig, derived.renderMetadata)
- useAssetOperations(provider, timelineId, userId, queryClient, save.pendingOpsRef)

Move orchestration logic from useTimelineData.ts:91-111:
- Create onSaveSuccessRef, wire to render.setRenderDirty
- Create pruneSelectionRef
- useLayoutEffect bridge: history.onBeforeCommit → save.onBeforeCommitRef

Note: useTimelineState already calls useDerivedTimeline a second time (line 60) with primaryClipId for multi-select — that second call remains.

Delete useTimelineData.ts. Run tests + tsc --noEmit.
tasks.executor_notes: Inlined the `useTimelineData` orchestration directly into `useTimelineState`: `useEditorPreferences`, `useTimelineQueries`, `useTimelineSave`, `useTimelineHistory`, the first `useDerivedTimeline`, `useRenderState`, and `useAssetOperations` now live in the state hook, and the wrapper-level bridges moved with them (`onSaveSuccessRef`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and the `useLayoutEffect` bridge wiring `history.onBeforeCommit` into `save.onBeforeCommitRef`). `useTimelineData.ts` was then deleted, and the second `useDerivedTimeline` call for `primaryClipId` remains in place. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed; `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` was re-run against the full module and reported one unrelated existing failure in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx`.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description:
Phase 4 Steps 10-11: Fix resolveGroupOverlaps meta patches and add adjustment feedback to resolveOverlaps.

Step 10: Modify resolveGroupOverlaps in resolve-overlaps.ts (line 200) to return { rows, metaPatches } instead of just rows. When gap-fitting shifts a timed clip's position, compute from/to meta adjustments using the same pattern as resolveOverlaps (lines 126-198). Remove the TODO at line 248.

Update applyMultiDragMoves in multi-drag-utils.ts to consume metaPatches. CRITICAL merge strategy: applyMultiDragMoves resolves overlaps inside a for...of loop over target rows and already builds metaUpdates with cross-track { track } patches. Must ACCUMULATE metaPatches across loop iterations using per-clip spread merge: { ...existing[clipId], ...overlapPatch } to preserve both track-move and trim adjustments.

Update useClipDrag.ts caller to pass accumulated meta patches through to applyEdit.

Step 11: Modify resolveOverlaps return type to include adjustments: Array<{ clipId, requestedStart, actualStart }>. Update callers to destructure the new shape. No callers use adjustments yet — this enables future UI feedback.

Add tests for BOTH resolveOverlaps AND resolveGroupOverlaps (entire module lacks direct tests). Create test file in __tests__ directory.

Run tests + tsc --noEmit.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `resolveOverlaps` to return `adjustments`, updated `resolveGroupOverlaps` to return `{ rows, metaPatches }`, and propagated overlap meta patches through `applyMultiDragMoves` using per-clip spread merge so cross-track `track` updates and overlap-derived `from`/`to` changes accumulate together. Updated callers to destructure the new overlap result shape and added `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tests__/resolve-overlaps.test.ts` covering `resolveOverlaps`, `resolveGroupOverlaps`, and multi-drag patch accumulation. Verification: `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` passed with 22 files / 69 tests, and `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.executor_notes: Simplified `useDerivedTimeline` by removing the signature-ref stabilization layer and deriving `resolvedConfig` directly from `data` with a `[data]`-scoped memo, which keeps the hook compliant with `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`. Verification passed with the full video-editor Vitest module, `tsc --noEmit`, and targeted ESLint on the changed files; no automated regression surfaced that required reverting the simplification.
commands_run: cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description:
Phase 6 Step 13: Improve AudioTrack key strategy. In AudioTrack.tsx, expand the composite key to include all audio-affecting properties:
```typescript
key={`${clip.id}-${clip.at}-${clip.from ?? 0}-${clip.to ?? ''}-${clip.speed ?? 1}`}
```
This ensures remount happens exactly when audio output would differ, not on unrelated prop changes.

Add a comment explaining WHY the composite key is needed (Remotion's declarative model requires remount for timing changes — Sequence + Audio components don't update timing from prop changes mid-playback).
tasks.executor_notes: Updated `reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/AudioTrack.tsx` so the `Sequence` key now includes `clip.id`, `clip.at`, `clip.from`, `clip.to`, and `clip.speed`, and added an inline comment explaining why Remotion audio timing changes require remount during playback. Verified the final source and ran `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`, which passed with 20 test files and 60 tests green.
commands_run: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

tasks.description:
Phase 6 Step 14: Final validation pass. Run in order:
1. Full video-editor test suite: `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/`
2. TypeScript compilation: `cd reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit`
3. Full app test suite: `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts`

Verify:
- Hook hierarchy is now max 2 layers: useTimelineState → {useTimelineCommit, useTimelinePersistence, usePollSync, useDerivedTimeline, useTimelineHistory, useRenderState, useAssetOperations, useEditorPreferences}
- useTimelineSave.ts is a thin composer (<150 lines)
- useTimelineData.ts is deleted; types preserved in useTimelineData.types.ts
- Single applyEdit replaces both old edit paths
- 3-context split (Editor/Chrome/Playback) unchanged

If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all pass.

watch_items: pendingOpsRef is used by BOTH useClipDrag (line 434) and useAssetOperations (lines 19-37). The isIdle formula must include pendingOpsRef === 0. Tests in useClipDrag.test.tsx:121-209 assert this contract.
createTrackAndMoveClip intentionally skips overlap resolution (new track = no neighbors). Under unified applyEdit it must use type:'config' path, preserving existing semantics exactly.
Phase 5 useMemo: repo enforces react-hooks/exhaustive-deps as ERROR. Do NOT use [data?.signature] as dep array — use [data] and compare signature internally.

Phase 4 multi-drag merge: applyMultiDragMoves resolves overlaps in a for...of loop and already builds metaUpdates with { track } patches. Must accumulate metaPatches across iterations with per-clip spread merge: { ...existing[clipId], ...overlapPatch }.
shouldAcceptPolledData re-export from useTimelineData.ts has zero consumers — explicitly drop it, don't let it disappear silently.
useDerivedTimeline is called TWICE: once with selectedClipId (from inlined useTimelineData), once with primaryClipId (from useTimelineState for multi-select). Both calls must remain.
resolve-overlaps.ts entirely lacks direct tests. Phase 4 should add tests for BOTH resolveOverlaps AND resolveGroupOverlaps, not just the group path.
DEBT: registry-patch-testing — patchRegistry() lacks automated regression tests. Do not make this worse.
DEBT: selection-save-bridge — the pruneSelection→useTimelineSave bridge needs careful handling when inlining useTimelineData into useTimelineState.
DEBT: multi-drag-utils — the meta patch accumulation concern from correctness-2/5 must be addressed in T7.
timeline-save-utils.test.ts (319 lines) is the critical regression suite for Phases 1-2. Pay extra attention after T2-T4.
All test commands must run from reigh-app/ directory: cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/

sense_checks.question: Does the dependency graph audit identify all shared refs between the three concern clusters, and are the proposed module interfaces sufficient to avoid circular dependencies?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. The audit identifies the full shared-ref surface across commit/edit, save/persist, and poll/sync (`dataRef`, `editSeqRef`, `savedSeqRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, `configVersionRef`, `lastSavedSignature`, `selectedClipIdRef`, `selectedTrackIdRef`, `isSavingRef`) and proposes a `scheduleSaveRef` bridge so the extracted modules can be composed without circular dependencies.

sense_checks.question: Do all existing tests pass after extraction? Does the isIdle derivation in usePollSync produce identical accept/reject decisions to the previous blacklist approach for every combination of editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOpsRef?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified with the full video-editor Vitest module suite and `npx tsc --noEmit`. `usePollSync.test.ts` exercises the full `editSeq/savedSeq/isSaving/pendingOps` matrix: when `isSaving=false`, the extracted gate matches the previous blacklist decisions; when `isSaving=true` and the other idle conditions are met, polls are intentionally rejected as `saving`, which is the approved default-reject tightening for this phase.

sense_checks.question: Is useTimelineSave.ts now <150 lines? Does its return type exactly match the previous interface so no downstream consumer breaks?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `useTimelineSave.ts` is 88 lines, composes `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync`, and still exports `SaveStatus`, `shouldAcceptPolledData`, and the same callable `useTimelineSave` surface used by `useTimelineData`. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed, so no downstream consumer broke.

sense_checks.question: Are ALL callers of applyTimelineEdit and applyResolvedConfigEdit migrated to applyEdit? Does createTrackAndMoveClip still use type:'config' and skip overlap resolution? Are the old wrappers fully removed?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. All video-editor callers now use `applyEdit`; `rg -n "applyTimelineEdit|applyResolvedConfigEdit" src/tools/video-editor` returns no remaining source usages. `createTrackAndMoveClip` still commits through the `type: 'config'` path in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`, so its existing no-overlap-resolution semantics are preserved, and the old wrappers were removed from the commit/data/context surfaces.

sense_checks.question: Do all 13+ importers of UseTimelineDataResult/ClipTab/SaveStatus/etc. now import from useTimelineData.types.ts? Does tsc --noEmit pass cleanly?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. The shared types now live in `useTimelineData.types.ts`, the planned importer sites now reference that module instead of `useTimelineData.ts`, and the old `ReturnType<typeof useTimelineData>` alias was replaced with the explicit `UseTimelineDataResult` type. `cd /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app && npx tsc --noEmit` passed cleanly.

sense_checks.question: Is useTimelineData.ts deleted? Are all three orchestration pieces (onSaveSuccessRef wiring, pruneSelectionRef, useLayoutEffect bridge) correctly moved to useTimelineState? Do both useDerivedTimeline calls remain?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, `useTimelineState.ts` now owns the three orchestration pieces that previously lived in the wrapper (`onSaveSuccessRef` wiring to `render.setRenderDirty`, `pruneSelectionRef`, and the `useLayoutEffect` bridge from `history.onBeforeCommit` to `save.onBeforeCommitRef`), and both `useDerivedTimeline` calls remain: one for `save.selectedClipId`/`save.selectedTrackId` and one for `primaryClipId` multi-select derivation. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed; the full video-editor Vitest module was also re-run and hit one unrelated existing failure in `ClipAction.test.tsx` outside this refactor path.

sense_checks.question: Does resolveGroupOverlaps now return metaPatches? Does applyMultiDragMoves correctly accumulate patches across loop iterations using per-clip spread merge? Are new tests added for BOTH resolveOverlaps and resolveGroupOverlaps?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `resolveGroupOverlaps` now returns `metaPatches`, `applyMultiDragMoves` accumulates overlap patches across loop iterations with per-clip spread merge so existing `track` patches are preserved, and new direct tests were added for both `resolveOverlaps` and `resolveGroupOverlaps` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/__tests__/resolve-overlaps.test.ts`. The full video-editor Vitest module passed after the change.

sense_checks.question: Does the simplified signature cache pass the lint rule react-hooks/exhaustive-deps? Does Remotion preview NOT flicker during editing? If it flickers, was the change reverted?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `useDerivedTimeline` now derives `resolvedConfig` with `useMemo(() => data?.resolvedConfig ?? null, [data])`, and the targeted ESLint pass on the changed files completed cleanly, so the `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` rule is satisfied. The full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` both passed after the simplification; no automated regression surfaced that required reverting the change.

sense_checks.question: Does AudioTrack key now include clip.id, clip.at, clip.from, clip.to, and clip.speed? Is there a comment explaining why the composite key is needed?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed. `AudioTrack` now keys each `Sequence` with `clip.id`, `clip.at`, `clip.from ?? 0`, `clip.to ?? ''`, and `clip.speed ?? 1`, and the file includes a comment explaining that Remotion audio timing and playback-rate changes require remount during playback.

sense_checks.question: Do ALL video-editor tests pass? Does tsc --noEmit succeed? Does the full app test suite pass? Is the hook hierarchy verified as max 2 layers deep?
sense_checks.executor_note: No. The video-editor suite passes (22 files / 71 tests) and `npx tsc --noEmit` succeeds, but the full app suite still fails in multiple unrelated shared/tooling areas outside the video-editor refactor. The structural checks confirm `useTimelineSave.ts` is a thin 87-line composer, `useTimelineData.ts` is deleted, the old edit-path symbols are gone, and the Editor/Chrome/Playback context split remains. The hook-hierarchy criterion is also not literally satisfied yet because `useTimelineState` still calls `useTimelineSave` rather than composing `useTimelineCommit`, `useTimelinePersistence`, and `usePollSync` directly.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance and gotchas:

**Ordering**: T1→T2→T3 (Phase 1) must complete before T4 (Phase 2). T5→T6 (Phase 3) depends on T4. T7 (Phase 4) depends on T4 but is independent of T5/T6 — can run in parallel with Phase 3. T8 (Phase 5) depends only on T3. T9 (Phase 6 audio) has no dependencies and can be done anytime. T10 is the final gate.

**Parallel opportunities**: T7, T8, and T9 can all be worked on in parallel after their respective dependencies are met.

**Biggest risk areas**:
1. The poll rejection inversion (T2/usePollSync) — must produce identical accept/reject decisions. Write the isIdle formula exactly as specified and verify against the existing getPollRejectionReason logic case by case.
2. The edit path unification (T4) touches 6+ hook files. Migrate one file at a time, running tests after each. The timeline-save-utils.test.ts (319 lines) is the critical regression suite.
3. Phase 3 inlining (T6) moves orchestration logic that's easy to get wrong — the useLayoutEffect bridge for history→save and the pruneSelectionRef wiring are subtle. Read useTimelineData.ts:91-111 very carefully before moving.
4. Phase 4 meta patch accumulation (T7) — the loop in applyMultiDragMoves processes multiple target rows. Each iteration's metaPatches must be MERGED into the accumulator, not replaced. Use spread: `metaUpdates[clipId] = { ...metaUpdates[clipId], ...overlapPatches[clipId] }`.

**Phase 5 lint trap**: The plan's sample useMemo code violates exhaustive-deps. Use [data] as dependency with internal comparison, or keep the existing ref pattern with cleaner variable names.

**applyResolvedConfigEdit is NOT on TimelineEditorContextValue** — it's only used internally by hooks. The context only exposes applyTimelineEdit (now applyEdit). This means the Phase 2 context migration only needs to replace one function on the type, not two.

**Test command**: Always `cd reigh-app && npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/` — the vitest config does not exist at workspace root.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Audit useTimelineSave internal dependency graph
Step 2: Extract useTimelinePersistence — save mutation, debounce, conflict retry
Step 3: Extract usePollSync — poll acceptance with default-reject
Step 4: Extract useTimelineCommit — commitData + edit functions + state
Step 5: Slim down useTimelineSave to thin composer
Step 8: Extract types and re-exports into shared types module
Step 9: Inline useTimelineData orchestration into useTimelineState
Step 10: Add metaPatches return to resolveGroupOverlaps
Step 11: Add adjustment feedback to resolveOverlaps
Step 13: Document and improve AudioTrack remount behavior

validation.completeness_notes: All 14 plan steps mapped to tasks. Steps 2-4 combined into T2 (they're tightly coupled extractions from the same file that must happen together). Steps 6-7 combined into T4 (design and migration are one logical unit). Steps 10-11 combined into T7 (both modify resolve-overlaps.ts). All other steps have 1:1 task mapping.
